Operation 'CreateApiKey' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details. Summary 'Create API keys' is too generic and plural. Description is comprehensive but lacks clear statement of what is returned. Improved summary specifies singular action with key detail about scopes. Enhanced description clarifies required/optional inputs, default behavior, return value including the critical detail that the key is only returned at creation, and the 100-key limit.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to create a new API Key for the user.**

To create your initial SendGrid API Key, you should [use the SendGrid App](https://app.sendgrid.com/settings/api_keys). Once you have created a first key with scopes to manage additional API keys, you can use this API for all other key management.
A JSON request body containing a `name` property is required when making requests to this endpoint. If the number of maximum keys, 100, is reached, a `403` status will be returned.
Though the `name` field is required, it does not need to be unique. A unique API key ID will be generated for each key you create and returned in the response body.
It is not necessary to pass a `scopes` field to the API when creating a key, but you should be aware that omitting the `scopes` field from your request will create a key with "Full Access" permissions by default.
See the [API Key Permissions List](https://docs.sendgrid.com/api-reference/how-to-use-the-sendgrid-v3-api/authorization) for all available scopes. An API key's scopes can be updated after creation using the "Update API keys" endpoint.
data.summary_suggestion: Create a new API key with optional scopes
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new API key for the authenticated user. Requires a JSON request body with a `name` property (required, does not need to be unique) and optional `scopes` array. If `scopes` is omitted, the key defaults to "Full Access" permissions. Returns the newly created API key object including the unique `api_key_id` and assigned scopes. Maximum of 100 keys per user; returns 403 if limit is reached. The returned key value is only available at creation time.
data.reasoning: Summary 'Create API keys' is too generic and plural. Description is comprehensive but lacks clear statement of what is returned. Improved summary specifies singular action with key detail about scopes. Enhanced description clarifies required/optional inputs, default behavior, return value including the critical detail that the key is only returned at creation, and the 100-key limit.

message: Operation 'UpdateApiKeyName' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details. Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description explains inputs well but lacks information about what is returned. Enhanced description adds return value details to improve AI agent understanding of the operation's complete effect.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to update the name of an existing API Key.**

You must pass this endpoint a JSON request body with a `name` property, which will be used to rename the key associated with the `api_key_id` passed in the URL.
data.description_suggestion: Updates the name of an existing API key. Requires the `api_key_id` in the URL path and a JSON request body with a `name` property. Returns the updated API key object with the new name, ID, and current scopes.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description explains inputs well but lacks information about what is returned. Enhanced description adds return value details to improve AI agent understanding of the operation's complete effect.

message: Operation 'UpdateApiKey' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details. Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description explains inputs and usage patterns well but lacks explicit statement of what is returned. Enhanced description clarifies that both fields are required, adds return value details, and reinforces the relationship to the name-only update endpoint for better AI agent decision-making.

data.current_summary: Update API key name and scopes
data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to update the name and scopes of a given API key.**

You must pass this endpoint a JSON request body with a `name` field and a `scopes` array containing at least one scope. The `name` and `scopes` fields will be used to update the key associated with the `api_key_id` in the request URL.

If you need to update a key's scopes only, pass the `name` field with the key's existing name; the `name` will not be modified. If you need to update a key's name only, use the "Update API key name" endpoint.

See the [API Key Permissions List](https://docs.sendgrid.com/api-reference/how-to-use-the-sendgrid-v3-api/authorization) for all available scopes.
data.description_suggestion: Updates the name and/or scopes of an existing API key. Requires the `api_key_id` in the URL path and a JSON request body with `name` (required) and `scopes` array (required, at least one scope). Both fields are updated together; to update only the name, use the "Update API key name" endpoint. Returns the updated API key object with the new name, ID, and scopes.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description explains inputs and usage patterns well but lacks explicit statement of what is returned. Enhanced description clarifies that both fields are required, adds return value details, and reinforces the relationship to the name-only update endpoint for better AI agent decision-making.

message: Operation 'DeleteApiKey' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary 'Delete API keys' is vague and plural; should specify singular action and use domain terminology 'revoke'. Description lacks output details (204 response) and doesn't clearly explain the permanent side effect. Enhanced summary uses 'revoke' terminology consistent with the description and specifies singular action. Enhanced description clarifies the permanent nature of the action, specifies the response code, explains the propagation delay side effect, and notes that a new key must be created to restore access.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to revoke an existing API Key using an `api_key_id`**

Authentications using a revoked API Key will fail after after some small propogation delay. If the API Key ID does not exist, a `404` status will be returned.
data.summary_suggestion: Revoke an API key by ID
data.description_suggestion: Revokes an existing API key, preventing further authentication attempts with that key. Requires the `api_key_id` in the URL path. Returns a 204 No Content response on success. Revoked keys will fail authentication after a brief propagation delay. Returns 404 if the API key ID does not exist. This action is permanent; a new API key must be created if access is needed again.
data.reasoning: Summary 'Delete API keys' is vague and plural; should specify singular action and use domain terminology 'revoke'. Description lacks output details (204 response) and doesn't clearly explain the permanent side effect. Enhanced summary uses 'revoke' terminology consistent with the description and specifies singular action. Enhanced description clarifies the permanent nature of the action, specifies the response code, explains the propagation delay side effect, and notes that a new key must be created to restore access.
